Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 10 percent year-over-year during the fourth quarter, with holiday season results reflecting strong performance in Amazon Web Services and advertising, partially offset by competitive consumer discretionary dynamics in North America and International retail.

Amazon Web Services revenue grew 21 percent year-over-year in the fourth quarter, reflecting accelerating demand for cloud infrastructure and artificial intelligence services driven by enterprise digital transformation and generative AI adoption at growing scale.

Advertising services revenue grew 19 percent year-over-year, easing from the third quarter rate as comparables strengthened, though underlying demand from brand and performance advertisers remained robust.

International segment turned to operating profitability for the first time, with cost discipline and growing Prime membership density that improves delivery economics driving the transition from the meaningful narrowing of operating losses seen in prior quarters.

We launched Trainium and Inferentia chip-powered instances in AWS to provide customers with purpose-built, cost-effective compute options for training and deploying large-scale machine learning models.

Item 3. Other Information
